OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
articles.description: Zero Shot, a new venture capital fund with deep ties to OpenAI, is aiming to raise $100 million for its first fund. It has already written some checks.

articles.title: Gas prices aren’t the only factor fueling used EV sales
articles.description: The most recent electric vehicle sales data provided a grim picture — at least for new EVs. Used EVs are moving in the opposite direction.

articles.title: Google quietly launched an AI dictation app that works offline
articles.description: Google's new offline-first dictation app uses Gemma AI models to take on the apps like Wispr Flow.

articles.title: Netflix is expanding into kids’ games with a new stand-alone app
articles.description: Netflix says the app gives children access to an "ever-growing" library of games for kids.

articles.title: Why safety regulators closed their investigation into Tesla’s remote parking feature
articles.description: The NHTSA closed its investigation into Tesla's "Actually Smart Summon" feature, saying that only a fraction of cases resulted in an incident and that no incidents resulted in injury. Tesla has also issued a number of software updates.

articles.title: Iran threatens ‘Stargate’ AI data centers
articles.description: Iran said it will target U.S.-linked data centers with new missile strikes, as the war between the U.S. and Iran escalates.

articles.title: Apple moves to take its App Store fight back to the Supreme Court
articles.description: Apple plans to ask the Supreme Court to review its App Store fight with Epic Games, as it challenges a ruling limiting its ability to charge fees on external payments.

articles.title: Watch this video of how a job interviewer exposes a North Korean fake IT worker
articles.description: An apparent North Korean worker was caught visibly stumped during a remote job interview when asked to insult the country's leader.

articles.title: North Korea’s hijack of one of the web’s most used open source projects was likely weeks in the making
articles.description: North Korean hackers pushed out malicious updates to a popular open source project by hacking a top developer's computer in a long-running campaign.

articles.title: OpenAI’s vision for the AI economy: public wealth funds, robot taxes, and a four-day workweek
articles.description: OpenAI proposes taxes on AI profits, public wealth funds, and expanded safety nets to address job loss and inequality, blending redistribution with capitalism as policymakers debate AI’s economic impact.

articles.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications open: a chance for VC access, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K
articles.description: Nominate your startup, or one you know that deserves the spotlight, and finish the process by applying. Selected 200 have a chance at VC access, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K for Startup Battlefield 200. Applications close on May 27.

articles.title: How to use the new ChatGPT app integrations, including DoorDash, Spotify, Uber, and others
articles.description: Learn how to use Spotify, Canva, Figma, Expedia, and other apps directly in ChatGPT.

articles.title: Ticket savings of up to $500 this week for TechCrunch Disrupt 2026
articles.description: Starting today, you have 5 days to save nearly $500 on your ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. This offer disappears Friday, April 10, at 11:59 p.m. PT. Register here to secure these low rates.

articles.title: Convicted spyware maker Bryan Fleming avoids jail at sentencing
articles.description: The pcTattletale founder escapes a custodial sentence following the first successful prosecution of a spyware maker in the U.S. in over a decade.

articles.title: Spain’s Xoople raises $130 million Series B to map the Earth for AI
articles.description: The company is also announcing a deal with L3Harris to build the sensors for Xoople's spacecraft.

articles.title: Xiaomi 17 Ultra pushes smartphone photography even further
articles.description: The Xiaomi 17 Ultra gives you a ton of options to play around with images, including preset filters and hardware add-ons.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### We Found a Ticking Time Bomb in macOS TCP Networking — It Detonates After Exactly 49 Days
URL: https://photon.codes/blog/we-found-a-ticking-time-bomb-in-macos-tcp-networking
Critical infrastructure bug with precise failure timeline discovered in Apple's networking stack.
A 49-day counter overflow in macOS TCP handling represents the kind of deterministic failure that enterprise IT fears most—systems that work perfectly until they suddenly don't. This affects long-running servers, dev machines, and any Mac that doesn't reboot regularly. Apple's response time and patch deployment will be closely watched by enterprise procurement.

### BrowserStack local leaking private key
URL: https://infosec.exchange/@badkeys/116359377342260172
Major testing infrastructure vendor exposed cryptographic credentials.
BrowserStack serves thousands of enterprise dev teams for cross-browser testing—a private key leak here could compromise CI/CD pipelines across the industry. This is the third significant security incident in dev tooling infrastructure this quarter. Supply chain security in developer tools remains an underpriced risk.

### Issue: Claude Code is unusable for complex engineering tasks with Feb updates
URL: https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/42796
Anthropic's coding assistant hitting capability walls on production-grade work.
User reports of degradation in complex engineering tasks suggest the gap between demo-ready and production-ready AI coding tools remains significant. This matters for evaluating the $18B+ valuations in AI coding assistants—customer churn signals are early indicators of commoditization risk. Watch for retention metrics from Cursor, Codeium, and competitors.

### A cryptography engineer's perspective on quantum computing timelines
URL: https://words.filippo.io/crqc-timeline/
Practitioner pushback on accelerated quantum threat timelines.
Filippo Valsorda is a serious voice in applied cryptography (formerly Google, Cloudflare). His skepticism on "Q-Day" urgency matters for post-quantum migration budgets—enterprises are being sold expensive upgrades on fear, not engineering reality. Useful calibration for anyone evaluating quantum security vendors.


## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### OpenAI alums have been quietly investing from a new, potentially $100M fund
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/06/openai-alums-have-been-quietly-investing-from-a-new-potentially-100m-fund/
Zero Shot fund signals talent exodus from OpenAI accelerating into capital deployment.
Former OpenAI employees raising $100M to back AI startups creates a fascinating arbitrage—they know what OpenAI can't or won't build. Deal flow from this fund will reveal capability gaps and strategic blind spots at the market leader. Pattern matches Stripe alumni fund playbook.

### AI singer now occupies eleven spots on iTunes singles chart
URL: https://www.showbiz411.com/2026/04/05/itunes-takeover-by-fake-ai-singer-eddie-dalton-now-occupies-eleven-spots-on-chart-despite-not-being-human-or-real-exclusive
Synthetic media breaking through distribution gatekeepers at scale.
"Eddie Dalton" holding 11 chart positions simultaneously demonstrates AI content is now indistinguishable enough to game discovery algorithms. This is an inflection point for music labels' catalog valuations and streaming platform liability. Expect regulatory response within 12 months.

### Iran threatens 'Stargate' AI data centers
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/06/iran-threatens-stargate-ai-data-centers/
Geopolitical risk now explicitly targeting AI infrastructure.
State actors identifying compute infrastructure as military targets changes insurance math and site selection for hyperscale buildouts. The $500B Stargate project's geographic concentration is a vulnerability that was theoretical last quarter—now it's on targeting lists. Watch for data center REIT repricing.

### Spain's Xoople raises $130 million Series B to map the Earth for AI
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/06/spains-xoople-raises-130-million-series-b-to-map-the-earth-for-ai/
European geospatial AI scaling with L3Harris defense partnership.
$130M for satellite-based earth observation data purpose-built for AI training suggests the synthetic data market is fragmenting by modality. L3Harris partnership signals defense applications are the real revenue driver. Competitive with Planet Labs and Umbra on the data side, but AI-native architecture may matter more.


## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### Sam Altman may control our future – can he be trusted?
URL: https://www.newyorker.com/magazine/2026/04/13/sam-altman-may-control-our-future-can-he-be-trusted
New Yorker long-form on OpenAI governance and Altman's consolidation of power.
Following the board drama, this profile will set narrative for DC policymakers and institutional LPs now asking harder questions about single points of failure in AI leadership. The timing—as OpenAI restructures toward for-profit—makes this required reading for anyone modeling the company's trajectory.

### North Korea's hijack of one of the web's most used open source projects was likely weeks in the making
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/06/north-koreas-hijack-of-one-of-the-webs-most-used-open-source-projects-was-likely-weeks-in-the-making/
State-sponsored supply chain attack via maintainer compromise.
Multi-week operation to compromise an individual maintainer, then push malicious updates—this is the XZ Utils playbook going mainstream. Every company running npm/pip/cargo dependencies is exposed. The unfunded labor model of open source security is now a national security problem.

### The Downfall and Enshittification of Microsoft in 2026
URL: https://caio.ca/blog/the-downfall-and-enshittification-of-microsoft.html
Technical deep-dive on Microsoft's product degradation across platforms.
The "enshittification" thesis applied to MSFT—worth reading for enterprise IT decision-makers weighing Azure commitment and licensing renewals. If accurate, this creates openings for competitors in dev tools, cloud, and productivity. Contrarian take on a $3T company.


## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The throughline this week is infrastructure vulnerability—from macOS networking bugs to supply chain compromises to literal missile threats against data centers. AI capability advances are plateauing into execution problems (see Claude Code issues) while distribution and security become the binding constraints. The OpenAI diaspora deploying capital, combined with talent dispersion, suggests the "winner take all" thesis in foundation models is weakening faster than valuations reflect.
